    17	function getVSCodePasteData(
    18	  clipboardData: DataTransfer,
    19	): { language: string } | null {
    20	  const raw = clipboardData.getData('vscode-editor-data')
    21	  if (!raw) return null
    22	  try {
    23	    const data = JSON.parse(raw)
    24	    return { language: data.mode || 'text' }
    25	  } catch {
    26	    return null
    27	  }
    28	}
    29	
    30	function hasRichHTML(clipboardData: DataTransfer): boolean {
    31	  const html = clipboardData.getData('text/html')
    32	  if (!html) return false
    33	  if (/data-vscode|vscode-/i.test(html)) return false
    34	  const doc = new DOMParser().parseFromString(html, 'text/html')
    35	  const richTags = doc.body.querySelectorAll(
    36	    'strong,em,b,i,h1,h2,h3,h4,h5,h6,ul,ol,table,img,blockquote,pre>code,a[href]',
    37	  )
    38	  return richTags.length > 0
    39	}
    40	
    41	function detectMarkdown(text: string): boolean {
    42	  let score = 0
    43	
    44	  if (/^#{1,6}\s+\S/m.test(text)) score += 5
    45	  if (/^```[\w-]*$/m.test(text)) score += 5
    46	  if (/\[.+?\]\(.+?\)/.test(text)) score += 4
    47	  if (/!\[.*?\]\(.+?\)/.test(text)) score += 5
    48	  if (/^\|.+\|$/m.test(text) && /^\|[-:\s|]+\|$/m.test(text)) score += 5
    49	  if (
    50	    /^>\s*\[!(?:NOTE|TIP|WARNING|CAUTION|IMPORTANT)\]/im.test(text)
    51	  )
    52	    score += 5
    53	  if (/^[-*]\s+\[[ x]\]/m.test(text)) score += 4
    54	
    55	  if (/\*\*.+?\*\*/.test(text)) score += 2
    56	  if (/(?<!\*)\*(?!\*)(?!\s).+?(?<!\s)(?<!\*)\*(?!\*)/.test(text)) score += 1
    57	  if (/^[-*+]\s+\S/m.test(text)) score += 1
    58	  if (/^\d+\.\s+\S/m.test(text)) score += 1
    59	  if (/^>\s+\S/m.test(text)) score += 1
    60	  if (/`.+?`/.test(text)) score += 1
    61	  if (/^[-*_]{3,}$/m.test(text)) score += 2
    62	
    63	  const paragraphs = text.split(/\n{2,}/).filter(Boolean)
    64	  if (paragraphs.length >= 3) score += 2
    65	
    66	  if (text.length < 20) score -= 3
    67	  if (!text.includes('\n')) score -= 2
    68	
    69	  return score >= 5
    70	}

    72	function convertAndInsert(markdown: string): void {

    19	  },

    21	  replace: (rootNode, _children, startMatch, _endMatch, linesInBetween) => {
    22	    const lang = startMatch[1] || ''
    23	    let code = ''
    24

    27	      while (lines.length > 0 && !lines[0].length) lines.shift()
    28	      while (lines.length > 0 && !lines.at(-1)?.length) lines.pop()

    37	    selection.add(node.getKey())

function detectMarkdown(text){
  let score=0
  if (/^#{1,6}\s+\S/m.test(text)) score += 5
  if (/^```[\w-]*$/m.test(text)) score += 5
  if (/\[.+?\]\(.+?\)/.test(text)) score += 4
  if (/!\[.*?\]\(.+?\)/.test(text)) score += 5
  if (/^\|.+\|$/m.test(text) && /^\|[-:\s|]+\|$/m.test(text)) score += 5
  if (/^>\s*\[!(?:NOTE|TIP|WARNING|CAUTION|IMPORTANT)\]/im.test(text)) score += 5
  if (/^[-*]\s+\[[ x]\]/m.test(text)) score += 4
  if (/\*\*.+?\*\*/.test(text)) score += 2
  if (/(?<!\*)\*(?!\*)(?!\s).+?(?<!\s)(?<!\*)\*(?!\*)/.test(text)) score += 1
  if (/^[-*+]\s+\S/m.test(text)) score += 1
  if (/^\d+\.\s+\S/m.test(text)) score += 1
  if (/^>\s+\S/m.test(text)) score += 1
  if (/`.+?`/.test(text)) score += 1
  if (/^[-*_]{3,}$/m.test(text)) score += 2
  const paragraphs=text.split(/\n{2,}/).filter(Boolean)
  if (paragraphs.length >= 3) score += 2
  if (text.length < 20) score -= 3
  if (!text.includes('\n')) score -= 2
  return {score,pass:score>=5}
}
const samples=[
'# Title',
'```js\nconsole.log(1)\n```',
'```\nx\n```',
'**bold**',
'[link](https://a.com)',
'- item\n- item2',
'> quote',
'This is plain text with *asterisk* maybe',
'line1\n\nline2\n\nline3',
]
for (const s of samples){
  console.log(JSON.stringify(s), detectMarkdown(s))
}
NODE
```

Output:
     1	# MarkdownPastePlugin Implementation Plan
     2	
     3	## Context
     4	
     5	The `@haklex/rich-editor` currently handles paste events only for images (via `ImageUploadPlugin`). When users paste Markdown text (e.g., from GitHub, documentation, or VS Code), it's inserted as plain text. This plugin auto-detects pasted Markdown and converts it to rich Lexical nodes, and also handles VS Code paste as code blocks.
     6	
     7	## Architecture
     8	
     9	### New File
    10	
    11	- `haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/MarkdownPastePlugin.tsx`
    12	
    13	### Modified Files
    14	
    15	- `haklex/rich-editor/src/components/RichEditor.tsx` — mount `<MarkdownPastePlugin />`
    16	- `haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/index.ts` — export plugin
    17	
    18	## Plugin Design
    19	
    20	Register `PASTE_COMMAND` at `COMMAND_PRIORITY_HIGH`. The handler follows this decision tree:
    21	
    22	```
    23	PASTE_COMMAND received
    24	├─ Has `vscode-editor-data` type?
    25	│  └─ YES → Create CodeBlock node with language + code → return true
    26	├─ Has `text/html` with real formatting?
    27	│  └─ YES → return false (let Lexical default HTML paste handle)
    28	├─ Get `text/plain`, run markdown detection
    29	│  ├─ score >= threshold → convert markdown → insert nodes → return true
    30	│  └─ score < threshold → return false (plain text paste)
    31	└─ No text → return false
    32	```
    33	
    34	### Step 1: VS Code Paste Detection
    35	
    36	```typescript
    37	function getVSCodePasteData(clipboardData: DataTransfer): { language: string } | null {
    38	  const raw = clipboardData.getData('vscode-editor-data')
    39	  if (!raw) return null
    40	  try {
    41	    const data = JSON.parse(raw)
    42	    return { language: data.mode || 'text' }
    43	  } catch {
    44	    return null
    45	  }
    46	}
    47	```
    48	
    49	When detected, split by double blank lines (`\n\n\n+`) into separate code blocks:
    50	
    51	```typescript
    52	const code = clipboardData.getData('text/plain')
    53	const segments = code.split(/\n{3,}/).filter(Boolean)
    54	const nodes = segments.map((seg) => $createCodeBlockEditNode(seg.trim(), language))
    55	$insertNodes(nodes)
    56	```
    57	
    58	Use `$createCodeBlockEditNode` (not `$createCodeBlockNode`) since we're in the editor context. Import from `../nodes/CodeBlockEditNode`. Single blank lines within code are preserved — only 2+ consecutive blank lines trigger splitting.
    59	
    60	### Step 2: Rich HTML Check
    61	
    62	```typescript
    63	function hasRichHTML(clipboardData: DataTransfer): boolean {
    64	  const html = clipboardData.getData('text/html')
    65	  if (!html) return false
    66	  // VS Code HTML contains data-vscode attributes — not "rich"
    67	  if (/data-vscode|vscode-/i.test(html)) return false
    68	  // Check for formatting tags beyond bare <p>/<span>
    69	  const doc = new DOMParser().parseFromString(html, 'text/html')
    70	  const richTags = doc.body.querySelectorAll(
    71	    'strong,em,b,i,h1,h2,h3,h4,h5,h6,ul,ol,table,img,blockquote,pre>code,a[href]'
    72	  )
    73	  return richTags.length > 0
    74	}
    75	```
    76	
    77	If `hasRichHTML` → `return false` to let Lexical's built-in HTML→Lexical conversion handle it.
    78	
    79	### Step 3: Markdown Detection (Scoring)
    80	
    81	```typescript
    82	function detectMarkdown(text: string): boolean {
    83	  let score = 0
    84	
    85	  // High confidence (5 pts each)
    86	  if (/^#{1,6}\s+\S/m.test(text)) score += 5          // ATX heading
    87	  if (/^```[\w-]*$/m.test(text)) score += 5            // Fenced code block
    88	  if (/\[.+?\]\(.+?\)/.test(text)) score += 4          // Link
    89	  if (/!\[.*?\]\(.+?\)/.test(text)) score += 5         // Image
    90	  if (/^\|.+\|$/m.test(text) &&
    91	      /^\|[-:\s|]+\|$/m.test(text)) score += 5         // Table
    92	  if (/^>\s*\[!(NOTE|TIP|WARNING|CAUTION|IMPORTANT)\]/mi.test(text)) score += 5
    93	  if (/^[-*]\s+\[[ x]\]/m.test(text)) score += 4      // Task list
    94	
    95	  // Medium confidence (2 pts each)
    96	  if (/\*\*.+?\*\*/.test(text)) score += 2             // Bold
    97	  if (/(?<!\*)\*(?!\*)(?!\s).+?(?<!\s)(?<!\*)\*(?!\*)/.test(text)) score += 1
    98	  if (/^[-*+]\s+\S/m.test(text)) score += 1            // Unordered list
    99	  if (/^\d+\.\s+\S/m.test(text)) score += 1            // Ordered list
   100	  if (/^>\s+\S/m.test(text)) score += 1                // Blockquote
   101	  if (/`.+?`/.test(text)) score += 1                    // Inline code
   102	  if (/^[-*_]{3,}$/m.test(text)) score += 2            // Horizontal rule
   103	
   104	  // Structural signals

_**Agent (gpt-5.3-codex 2026-03-03 16:52:33Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Assessing paste handling and markdown plugin risks**
</details></think>

_**Agent (gpt-5.3-codex 2026-03-03 16:52:46Z)**_

**Findings**
1. **High**: VS Code code paste can corrupt code indentation/content due `trim()`.  
[haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/MarkdownPastePlugin.tsx:121](<REPO_ROOT>/haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/MarkdownPastePlugin.tsx:121), [haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/MarkdownPastePlugin.tsx:123](<REPO_ROOT>/haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/MarkdownPastePlugin.tsx:123)  
`segments.map((s) => $createCodeBlockEditNode(s.trim(), ...))` removes leading whitespace on the first line and trailing newlines. Pasting an indented Python/YAML/code fragment can become syntactically wrong.

2. **High**: Markdown paste can be silently dropped on conversion/parsing failure.  
[haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/MarkdownPastePlugin.tsx:76](<REPO_ROOT>/haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/MarkdownPastePlugin.tsx:76), [haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/MarkdownPastePlugin.tsx:138](<REPO_ROOT>/haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/MarkdownPastePlugin.tsx:138), [haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/MarkdownPastePlugin.tsx:139](<REPO_ROOT>/haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/MarkdownPastePlugin.tsx:139)  
`event.preventDefault()` is called before conversion, and temp editor errors are swallowed (`onError: () => {}`). If conversion/parse fails, default paste is already blocked and user gets nothing inserted.

3. **Medium**: Markdown detector is overly strict and misses common markdown inputs.  
[haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/MarkdownPastePlugin.tsx:44](<REPO_ROOT>/haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/MarkdownPastePlugin.tsx:44), [haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/MarkdownPastePlugin.tsx:66](<REPO_ROOT>/haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/MarkdownPastePlugin.tsx:66), [haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/MarkdownPastePlugin.tsx:67](<REPO_ROOT>/haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/MarkdownPastePlugin.tsx:67), [haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/MarkdownPastePlugin.tsx:69](<REPO_ROOT>/haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/MarkdownPastePlugin.tsx:69)  
Examples like `# Title`, short fenced blocks, simple lists, and single markdown links score below threshold and won’t convert, which is likely surprising for “markdown text paste”.

4. **Medium**: VS Code segment splitting is brittle for real-world text.  
[haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/MarkdownPastePlugin.tsx:121](<REPO_ROOT>/haklex/rich-editor/src/plugins/MarkdownPastePlugin.tsx:121)  
Splitting on `\n{3,}` can incorrectly split valid code containing larger blank gaps, and it won’t split Windows `\r\n` multi-segment content.

**Open questions / assumptions**
1. Should *all* `vscode-editor-data` pastes be forced into code blocks, including `mode: "markdown"` and prose files?
2. Do you want single-line markdown (headings/links/lists) to convert, or intentionally remain plain text?
